title: Create a Smart Dialer Campaign
lede: Launch an AI-powered outbound calling machine: create a campaign, give it an AI agent and phone numbers, load the people to call, tune the pacing and schedule, and press Start. Your agent does the rest — around the clock, within the hours you allow.

steps.title: Open the Campaigns list
steps.content:
Everything starts on the Smart Dialer list — your command center for every AI calling campaign you run.

  1. In the top navigation bar, click Campaigns.
  2. In the row of sub-tabs that appears, make sure Smart Dialer is selected (it is the default).

The table shows one row per campaign with these columns:

  • Status — Draft (never started), Running, After hours (waiting for your working-hours window), Cooling down (waiting out the retry delay), Paused, Stopped, Completed, or Archived. Use the filter icon on the column header to filter by status.
  • Contacts — how many people are queued in the campaign's dial list.
  • CPS — the campaign's Max Concurrent Calls setting (calls placed simultaneously).
  • Progress — dial attempts made vs. total attempts available (hover the bar for exact numbers).
  • Total Cost — all-time call spend for the campaign.
  • Actions — 👁 view, ▶ start / ⏸ pause, and 🗑 delete shortcuts.

Above the table you can Filter by label, Filter by owner, and switch between List and Folders view — Folders groups campaigns by the labels you assign them, which is a lifesaver once you run dozens.

images.alt: Smart Dialer campaigns list with status, contacts, CPS, progress and actions columns
images.caption: The Smart Dialer list — Create New Campaign lives in the top-right

images.alt: Smart Dialer campaigns list with status, contacts, CPS, progress and actions columns
images.caption: The Smart Dialer list — Create New Campaign lives in the top-right

steps.title: Create the campaign and name it
steps.content:
1. Click Create New Campaign in the top-right of the list.
  2. A new campaign is created instantly and opens in a side panel. It starts in Draft status — nothing dials until you press Start, so you can configure everything at your own pace.
  3. Click the campaign's title at the top of the panel (e.g. "Campaign #238") to rename it. Type the new name and press Enter.

The panel opens on the Overview tab: live tiles for Contacts, Attempts Made, Attempts Left, Finished, Progress and Live Calls, plus All-Time Call History, Next Scheduled Calls and Recent Activity. It's empty now — by the end of this guide it will be alive with data.

💡 The header also holds your controls for later: Start / Pause, Export CSV, Export PDF, and the red trash icon to delete the campaign.

images.alt: New draft campaign open on the Overview tab with stat tiles and the Start button
images.caption: A fresh draft campaign — the Overview tab and the Start button in the header

images.alt: New draft campaign open on the Overview tab with stat tiles and the Start button
images.caption: A fresh draft campaign — the Overview tab and the Start button in the header

steps.title: Attach an AI agent and caller numbers (Settings tab)
steps.content:
Open the Settings tab. Every change here saves automatically — there is no Save button to forget.

Under Basic Information:

  • Name / Description — keep these meaningful so you can tell campaigns apart later.

Under Call Settings:

  • Phone Numbers — the numbers your campaign dials from. Numbers are grouped by label; expand a group with the arrow to pick individual numbers, or hit Select all next to a group to grab the whole set. Selecting several numbers spreads your outbound volume across them.
  • AI Agents — the Smart Agent that speaks to every person who answers. You can search the dropdown by name. No agent yet? Build one first — see the Create an AI Agent guide, then come back.
  • Max Concurrent Calls — how many calls run at the same time (this is the CPS column on the list). Up to 10; if you need more, contact support.
  • User Do Not Call List — toggle ON to automatically apply your account-wide Do Not Call list to this campaign, so blocked numbers are never dialed.
  • Local Presence Dialing — toggle ON and the campaign automatically dials each contact from the outbound number whose area code best matches theirs. Familiar-looking numbers get answered dramatically more often. When enabled, a Country Code Hint field appears to help match numbers correctly for your market.

images.alt: Campaign Settings tab showing Basic Information and Call Settings with phone number groups and AI agent picker
images.caption: Settings tab — pick caller numbers by group and attach your AI agent

images.alt: Campaign Settings tab showing Basic Information and Call Settings with phone number groups and AI agent picker
images.caption: Settings tab — pick caller numbers by group and attach your AI agent

steps.title: Decide how persistent the dialer should be (Retry Settings)
steps.content:
Scroll down the Settings tab to the Retry Settings card. This is where you control follow-up attempts for people who don't answer:

  • Max Dials Per Phone Number — the maximum times each phone number is attempted (e.g. 3 means one call plus up to two retries).
  • Retry Delay (minutes) — how long the dialer waits between attempts to the same number. While it waits, the campaign shows the Cooling down status.
  • All Numbers for Lead/Contact Stop After — pick one or more stages; when a lead or contact reaches any of them (for example Interested or Do Not Call), the campaign stops calling every number that person has.
  • Individual Number Stop Retrying — same idea, but only stops the specific phone number that hit the stage; the person's other numbers stay in the queue.
💡 Stage-based stops are what make the dialer feel smart: your AI agent updates a person's stage during the call, and the campaign instantly stops chasing people who already said yes — or no.

images.alt: Retry Settings card with max dials per phone number, retry delay and stage-based stop rules
images.caption: Retry Settings — attempts, delay between retries, and stage-based stop rules

images.alt: Retry Settings card with max dials per phone number, retry delay and stage-based stop rules
images.caption: Retry Settings — attempts, delay between retries, and stage-based stop rules

steps.title: Set the calling window (Working Hours)
steps.content:
Still on the Settings tab, find the Working Hours card. The campaign only places calls inside this window — outside it, the status shows After hours and dialing resumes automatically the next window.

  • Start Time / End Time — e.g. 09:00 to 17:00.
  • Timezone — leave it on Use Contacts/Leads Timezones and each person is called in their own local window (the polite, high-connect-rate choice). Or pin a specific zone (Eastern, Pacific, London, Sydney, …) to run the whole campaign on one clock.
  • Working Days — check the days calls are allowed. Weekdays are checked by default; add Sat/Sun only if your audience expects it.
⚠️ Know your local telemarketing rules — many regions restrict cold-calling hours. The default 09:00–17:00, Monday–Friday window is a safe starting point.

images.alt: Working Hours card with start and end time, timezone selector and working day checkboxes
images.caption: Working Hours — the campaign dials only inside this window

images.alt: Working Hours card with start and end time, timezone selector and working day checkboxes
images.caption: Working Hours — the campaign dials only inside this window

steps.title: Catch the callbacks (Inbound Callbacks)
steps.content:
People you missed will often call your number back — don't let those hot returns go to a generic greeting. The Inbound Callbacks card (on the Settings tab) routes them intelligently while the campaign is running, and it even works when several campaigns share the same number: the callback is matched to the campaign that actually dialed that person.

  1. Toggle Handle callbacks while running ON. Two more options appear:
  • Ring a team group (humans) — toggle ON to ring the online members of a permission group first (first to accept wins). A Group to ring dropdown appears — pick the team. Leave it OFF to have an AI agent answer directly.
  • When a customer calls back (labelled If nobody's available when a group is ringing) — choose what happens:
    • The campaign's AI agent answers — the same agent that made the original call picks up.
    • A specific AI agent answers — an AI agent for callbacks dropdown appears; pick any agent (great for a dedicated "callback" persona).
    • Let the number's default AI agent answer — falls through to the number's normal inbound routing.
    • Drop the call — decline the callback (only available when no group is ringing).

images.alt: Inbound Callbacks card expanded showing the handle callbacks toggle, ring a team group option and callback policy dropdown
images.caption: Inbound Callbacks — route returned calls to your team or an AI agent

images.alt: Inbound Callbacks card expanded showing the handle callbacks toggle, ring a team group option and callback policy dropdown
images.caption: Inbound Callbacks — route returned calls to your team or an AI agent

steps.title: Load the people to call (Add Leads / Add Contacts)
steps.content:
Time to fuel the machine. Campaigns pull straight from your CRM — no CSV gymnastics required.

  1. Open the Add Leads tab (for CRM leads) or the Add Contacts tab (for CRM contacts). Both work identically.
  2. Click Filters to find exactly who you want: search by Name, Phone or Email, or filter by Lifecycle Stage, Source, Account, City, Tags, State/Province, Country or Zip/Postal Code. Combine as many as you like.
  3. Tick the checkbox next to each person, or use the header checkbox to select the whole page. When a full page is selected you'll be offered Select all entries to grab every match across all pages.
  4. Click Add Selected Leads (n) (or Add Selected Contacts (n)). Everyone is queued into the campaign's dial list.

Use Refresh to reload the table, and click any name or View to peek at the full CRM record without leaving the campaign.

💡 Only people with a phone number can be dialed — records without one are skipped. If your list lives outside iMash, import it into the CRM first (see Import & Export Data), then add it here with one filtered select-all.

images.alt: Add Leads tab with lead rows, checkboxes and the Add Selected Leads button
images.caption: Add Leads — tick people and click Add Selected Leads to queue them

images.alt: Add Leads tab with lead rows, checkboxes and the Add Selected Leads button
images.caption: Add Leads — tick people and click Add Selected Leads to queue them

images.alt: Filters dropdown on the Add Leads tab showing search by name, lifecycle stage, source, account, city, tags, state, country and zip filters
images.caption: The Filters dropdown — slice your CRM by stage, source, tags, geography and more

images.alt: Filters dropdown on the Add Leads tab showing search by name, lifecycle stage, source, account, city, tags, state, country and zip filters
images.caption: The Filters dropdown — slice your CRM by stage, source, tags, geography and more

steps.title: Review the queue (Dial List tab)
steps.content:
Open the Dial List tab to see everyone queued, one row per phone number:

  • Entity Name — click it to open the full lead/contact record.
  • Status — Pending (still to be dialed) or completed.
  • Global Stage — the person's current lifecycle stage.
  • Attempts — dials made vs. your Max Dials setting (e.g. 0/3).
  • Target Phone, Call Stage and Scheduled At — which number will be dialed and when the next attempt is due.

Tools above the table:

  • Show All / Show Pending / Show Completed — quick status filters.
  • Search by name or phone number and Filter by lifecycle stages — find specific entries; Clear Filters resets both.
  • Delete Selected (n) — tick rows (or select all across pages) and remove them from the queue. This only removes them from the campaign — the CRM records are untouched.
  • Refresh — reload the list.

images.alt: Dial List tab showing queued entries with status, stage, attempts, target phone and scheduled time
images.caption: The Dial List — every queued number with its attempt count and schedule

images.alt: Dial List tab showing queued entries with status, stage, attempts, target phone and scheduled time
images.caption: The Dial List — every queued number with its attempt count and schedule

steps.title: Optional: give the campaign its own stages (Stages tab)
steps.content:
By default the campaign uses your global CRM lifecycle stages. If this campaign needs its own outcome labels (say, Booked Demo or Wrong Number):

  1. Open the Stages tab.
  2. Toggle Use custom stages for this campaign ON. The editor pre-fills with a copy of your global stages.
  3. Add, rename, or remove stages, then click Save Campaign Stages.

Your AI agent will classify calls into these stages, they'll appear across the campaign's tables and reports, and they become the options in the Retry Settings stop rules. Toggle the switch OFF at any time to revert to the global stages.

images.alt: Stages tab with the use custom stages toggle and info banner about global CRM stages
images.caption: Stages tab — flip the toggle to define campaign-specific stages

images.alt: Stages tab with the use custom stages toggle and info banner about global CRM stages
images.caption: Stages tab — flip the toggle to define campaign-specific stages

steps.title: Press Start — and watch it work
steps.content:
This is the moment. Double-check your agent, numbers, hours and list, then:

  1. Click Start in the campaign header (or the ▶ icon on the campaign's row in the list).
  2. The status flips to Running and dialing begins — immediately if you're inside the working-hours window, otherwise at the next window (After hours).
  3. Watch it live: the Overview tab updates in real time, and the Campaign Logs tab streams every call as it ends — date, ended reason, duration, who was called, cost, and any stage transition the AI made. Click a row for the full call detail, transcript and recording.
  4. Click Pause any time to stop dialing; calls already in progress finish gracefully. Press Start again to resume.

Need the data elsewhere? Export CSV / Export PDF in the header download the full campaign log.

When every number has been fully attempted, the campaign flips to Completed automatically and its dial list becomes a read-only archive.

⚠️ Housekeeping: paused campaigns idle for ~30 days (drafts ~15) get flagged with a yellow Will archive warning, then auto-archived a few days later. Open the campaign and click Resume to keep it going, or Pin (don't auto-archive) to keep it parked indefinitely.

images.alt: Campaign Logs tab with real-time call log columns for date, ended reason, duration, cost and stage transition
images.caption: Campaign Logs — every call streams in here in real time once you hit Start

images.alt: Campaign Logs tab with real-time call log columns for date, ended reason, duration, cost and stage transition
images.caption: Campaign Logs — every call streams in here in real time once you hit Start

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaTitle: Create a Smart Dialer Campaign | iMash Documentation
metaDescription: Step-by-step guide to launching an AI outbound calling campaign in iMash: attach an AI agent and caller numbers, load leads and contacts, set retries, working hours and inbound callbacks, then start and monitor it live.
